Problem 378: Triangle Triples

Let $T(n)$ be the nth triangle number, so $T(n) = \dfrac{n(n + 1)}{2}$. Let $dT(n)$ be the number of divisors of $T(n)$. E.g.: $T(7) = 28$ and $dT(7) = 6$. Let $Tr(n)$ be the number of triples $(i, j, k)$ such that $1 \le i \lt j \lt k \le n$ and $dT(i) \gt dT(j) \gt dT(k)$. $Tr(20) = 14$, $Tr(100) = 5772$, and $Tr(1000) = 11174776$. Find $Tr(60 000 000)$. Give the last 18 digits of your answer.

Hint: 
1. Break down the problem into smaller steps
2. Consider mathematical properties that might simplify the solution
3. Think about efficient algorithms to solve this type of problem
